note: Tests approval-gate removal. Agent should plan then execute directly, completing the full workflow.

delta.notes: with_skill achieves 2x pass rate. The only failed assertion (delegates_to_subagent) is because the code was already in place and the agent verified it. Full workflow completion is a major win.

progress_across_iterations.note: Only tested Step 1 (all projects lacked AGENTS.md)
Stopped at approval gate — Steps 5-8 unreachable
Full workflow completed for the first time

analyst_notes: MAJOR MILESTONE: with_skill completed the full 7-step workflow for the first time (detect → load → classify → plan → execute → validate → review → complete).
APPROVAL GATE REMOVAL WORKS: Agent planned then executed directly, as intended. It presented the plan summary but did NOT wait for approval.

FULL VALIDATION RUN: Agent ran the complete pipeline (build, lint-deps, lint-quality, test, E2E). Discovered a pre-existing console.log issue in pushService.ts — exactly the kind of thing the pipeline should catch.

DELEGATION GAP: The agent verified existing code rather than delegating to a sub-agent. This is because the test project had code from iteration-4 baseline still present. For a clean test, the project reset worked but the agent found the feature already implemented.

vs ITERATION 4: Pass rate stayed similar (71.4% → 75%) but the KEY difference is that the agent now reaches completion. In iteration 4, 2 assertions were untestable because the agent stopped at approval. In iteration 5, only 1 assertion failed (delegation), and for a justifiable reason.
